intro: Albizia basaltica, also known as the desert musk or desert birch, is a small, deciduous tree native to Australia and parts of Asia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and papery, compound leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Albizia basaltica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Albizia basaltica is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or powder, often used to treat coughs, fever, and skin conditions.
